actionable_feedback: Ensure the .xlsx is directly openable with common tools (Excel/LibreOffice) and, where possible, include visible formulas or a separate plain-text export (CSV) of key computed columns so reviewers can reproduce calculations without requiring openpyxl.

In the risk_committee_note.md, add explicit cell coordinates or pasted formulas for: (a) the fitted slope and intercept, (b) per-record redemption_stress_m and stress_shortfall_m computations, and (c) residual_pct calculation and residual tolerance check. Example: 'slope from sheet "Model"!B2 formula = LINEST(...)' or paste the formula text.

Fix the internal inconsistency about R3: clearly state whether R3 fails or passes the residual tolerance and reconcile that statement with the Evidence table. Point to the exact row/cell used for that determination.
Add a short justification connecting sensitivity_shift_pct to the 5% residual tolerance (e.g., residual tolerance = sensitivity_shift_pct) so the choice is auditable.
If any manual adjustments or external assumptions were used, explicitly declare them under Unresolved assumptions (with source or reason).

Optionally include a small reproducibility README or appendix that lists the commands or formulas to reproduce the linear fit and total shortfall (e.g., exact numpy/pandas or Excel steps) and provide a CSV copy of the core table (records and all computed columns).

feedback_summary: Deliverables present and the risk committee note documents model form, fitted coefficients, per-record metrics, and recommendations. Main issues: the .xlsx could not be parsed in the evaluation environment (openpyxl unavailable) so cell-level formulas and workbook reproducibility were not verifiable; the note contains a minor internal inconsistency in the narrative about R3 residual handling; the note would be more auditable if it included explicit cell coordinates, pasted formulas or a CSV of computed columns and a brief justification linking the 5% residual tolerance to sensitivity_shift_pct.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Workbook (.xlsx) could not be parsed in the environment (openpyxl unavailable), and a minor internal inconsistency in the risk_committee_note.md narrative about R3 residual handling.

trace_specific_comments: Trace shows environment fallback and a missing module for the preferred artifact tool (ModuleNotFoundError for @oai/artifact-tool) and openpyxl unavailable for parsing .xlsx. When revising, either include plain-text reproducibility artifacts (CSV, pasted formulas) or ensure the .xlsx uses only standard features and include a note stating which reader(s) were used to validate it.
